content.content:
[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2970184212","pull_request_review_id":3986759902,"id":2970184212,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk86xCWoU","diff_hunk":"@@ -104,6 +104,16 @@
     color-scheme = 1; # 1 = prefer dark
   };
 
+  xdg.configFile."chrome-flags.conf".text = ''
+    --ozone-platform=wayland
+    --enable-features=WaylandWindowDecorations
+  '';
+
+  xdg.configFile."electron-flags.conf".text = ''
+    --ozone-platform=wayland
+    --enable-features=WaylandWindowDecorations
+  '';","path":"home/workstation.nix","commit_id":"2571c4d9ba554763da9fa8d961be9d70eb7d8376","original_commit_id":"2571c4d9ba554763da9fa8d961be9d70eb7d8376","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Since `chrome-flags.conf` and `electron-flags.conf` have identical content, you can avoid duplication by defining the file once and creating a symbolic link for the other. This follows the Don't Repeat Yourself (DRY) principle and simplifies future updates. You can use the `source` attribute to achieve this.

```
  xdg.configFile."electron-flags.conf".source = config.xdg.configFile."chrome-flags.conf".source;
```","created_at":"2026-03-21T20:32:32Z","updated_at":"2026-03-21T20:32:33Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/372#discussion_r2970184212","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/372","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2970184212"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/372#discussion_r2970184212"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/372"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2970184212/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":112,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":null,"original_line":115,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":12,"position":1,"subject_type":"line"},{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2970184213","pull_request_review_id":3986759902,"id":2970184213,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk86xCWoV","diff_hunk":"@@ -34,6 +34,7 @@

     NIXOS_OZONE_WL = "1";
+    _JAVA_AWT_WM_NONREPARENTING = "1";","path":"modules/common/workstation.nix","commit_id":"2571c4d9ba554763da9fa8d961be9d70eb7d8376","original_commit_id":"2571c4d9ba554763da9fa8d961be9d70eb7d8376","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

With the addition of this Java-specific variable, the comment on line 34 (`# Hint Electron apps to use Wayland`) has become incomplete. To maintain clarity, I suggest adding a comment to explain this new variable's purpose. It would also be a good idea to update the block comment on line 34 to be more encompassing, such as `# Session variables for Wayland compatibility`.

```
    _JAVA_AWT_WM_NONREPARENTING = "1"; # For JetBrains IDE compatibility on Wayland
